title: Gingidium decipiens
intro: Gingidium decipiens is a species of plant in the family Euphorbiaceae, commonly known as the "Malayan croton" or "Malay croton." The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate conditions such as fever, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of G. decipiens include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are often used to treat various ailments in traditional medicine.
taxonomy: Gingidium decipiens belongs to the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the madder family. This family comprises over 1,000 genera and 60,000 known species. The Rubiaceae family is widely distributed across the globe, with members found in tropical, subtropical, and temperate regions. Within the family Rubiaceae, Gingidium decipiens is classified as a species of flowering plant. Further classification of Gingidium decipiens includes its placement within the tribe Naucleeae.
